question: Who built this site?
answer: A new student at Kyambogo University built this site to help fellow students access study resources easily. It's a passion project to make learning more accessible for everyone.

question: Is this site affiliated with Kyambogo University?
answer: No, this is a student-built resource hub and is not officially affiliated with Kyambogo University. It is created by a student for students.

question: Which courses are covered on this site?
answer: We currently cover BITC (Bachelor of Information Technology & Computing), BEEM (Bachelor of Environmental Engineering & Management), and Nursing programs. We're working to add more courses from other schools at Kyambogo University.

question: What resources are available on this site?
answer: We offer past papers with answer guides, practice quizzes with instant feedback, study guides, and course notes. New resources are added regularly.

question: Are the past papers official?
answer: Past papers are sourced from various channels and are shared for educational purposes. We encourage students to verify with their course lecturers for the most accurate information.

question: How do I take a quiz?
answer: Navigate to the course unit page, select the MCQ module, and click on an episode to start the quiz. You'll receive instant feedback on your answers.

question: Do I need an account to use this site?
answer: Currently, you can access all resources without an account. In the future, we may add user accounts to track progress and save quiz results.

question: How do I search for resources?
answer: Use the search bar on the home page or any page with search functionality. You can search by course name, topic, or resource type.

question: Is the site mobile-friendly?
answer: Yes! The site is fully responsive and works on all devices — phones, tablets, and desktops.

question: How can I contribute resources?
answer: Visit our Contribute page to submit past papers, notes, or quizzes. You'll be credited for your contributions and help fellow students!

question: What types of resources can I contribute?
answer: You can contribute past papers, study notes, practice quizzes, cheat sheets, formula lists, and any other helpful study materials.

question: Will I be credited for my contributions?
answer: Yes! Your name will appear on the resource page as a contributor. You can also share your GitHub or social links if you'd like.
